title: Honeycomb Event
description: Represents a telemetry event sent to Honeycomb via the Events API. Events are the fundamental unit of data in Honeycomb, containing structured key-value pairs that describe an operation or occurrence in your system.

time.description: The event's timestamp in ISO8601 or RFC3339 format. Defaults to the server's current time if not provided.

samplerate.description: An integer representing the sampling denominator. A value of 10 means one in ten events was kept during sampling.

data.description: The event payload containing custom fields as key-value pairs. Fields become columns in the Honeycomb dataset.

trace.trace_id.description: The trace identifier linking related spans together.

trace.span_id.description: The unique identifier for this span within a trace.

trace.parent_id.description: The span ID of the parent span in a trace.

service_name.description: The name of the service that generated this event.

name.description: The name of the operation or event.

duration_ms.description: The duration of the operation in milliseconds.

error.description: Indicates whether the operation resulted in an error.

http.method.description: The HTTP method of the request.

http.route.description: The matched route pattern for the HTTP request.

http.url.description: The full URL of the HTTP request.

BatchEventResponse.description: Response for an individual event within a batch submission.

status.description: The HTTP status code for this individual event. 202 indicates successful queuing.

error.description: An error message if the event failed processing.
